overview: Tarkir: Dragonstorm (TDS) visits a timeline where the Dragonstorms have returned in full force. The set focuses on a hybrid of 3-color clan identities and 2-color mechanical synergies. Key themes include graveyard manipulation (Renew), token production (Mobilize), and spell-doubling (Harmonize). The format is high-power with significant rewards for casting multiple spells per turn.

mechanics.name: Mobilize
mechanics.description: Creates additional creature tokens to aid in your attacks. Often triggers on ETB or attack.
mechanics.draft_tip: Mobilize tokens are great for sacrifice fodder in BR or go-wide strategies in RW. Don't value them as individual cards, but as resources.

mechanics.name: Harmonize
mechanics.description: Allows you to cast your spells twice or gain bonuses based on your biggest creatures.
mechanics.draft_tip: Harmonize is mana-intensive. Prioritize ramp or cost-reduction to ensure you can actually cast the spell twice.

mechanics.name: Flurry
mechanics.description: Rewards you for casting two or more spells in a single turn.
mechanics.draft_tip: Draft cheap 1-mana cantrips and spells with 'Two Spells' synergies to maximize your Flurry triggers every turn.

mechanics.name: Renew
mechanics.description: Uses the power of the graveyard to make your creatures on the battlefield stronger.
mechanics.draft_tip: Self-mill is essential. If you don't have a way to fill your graveyard, Renew cards will stay below-average in stats.

archetypes.name: Abzan Endure
archetypes.description: Outsize your opponent with +1/+1 counters or outflank them with Spirit tokens.
archetypes.strategy: Play a defensive early game with Spirits, then use +1/+1 counters to make your small flyers into massive threats.

archetypes.name: Jeskai Flurry
archetypes.description: Cast two (or more) spells in a single turn to overwhelm your opponent.
archetypes.strategy: Tempo is everything. Use cheap removal and card draw to trigger Flurry every turn while chipping away with Prowess creatures.

archetypes.name: Sultai Renew
archetypes.description: Use the power of the graveyard to make your creatures stronger.
archetypes.strategy: Fill your graveyard early. Focus on creatures that gain power based on the number of card types in your yard.

archetypes.name: Mardu Mobilize
archetypes.description: Create additional tokens to aid in your attacks.
archetypes.strategy: Aggro and go-wide. Use Mobilize tokens to ensure you always have more attackers than the opponent has blockers.

archetypes.name: Temur Harmonize
archetypes.description: Cast your spells twice with help from your biggest creatures.
archetypes.strategy: Midrange ramp. Play 4+ power creatures to enable your Harmonize payoffs and double your value in the late game.

archetypes.name: Sacrifice
archetypes.description: Sacrifice your extra creatures, such as temporary ones from mobilize, to get extra use out of those creatures.
archetypes.strategy: Turn your 1/1 tokens into damage and cards. Use sacrifice outlets to dodge exile removal.

archetypes.name: Two Spells
archetypes.description: Use harmonize spells frequently in order to reap the rewards from Flurry.
archetypes.strategy: Hyper-synergy. By doubling your spells with Harmonize, you automatically trigger Flurry with a single card's effect.

archetypes.name: Counters
archetypes.description: Use counters of any kind, not just +1/+1 counters, to make your creatures formidable in combat.
archetypes.strategy: Build one or two massive 'voltron' creatures that the opponent cannot block or kill easily.

archetypes.name: Go Wide Tokens
archetypes.description: Blitz by your opponent with sheer numbers. Use mobilize tokens and flurry rewards to build impressive battlefields.
archetypes.strategy: Fastest deck in the format. Flood the board and use team-wide pump spells to end the game by turn 5.

archetypes.name: Leaves the Graveyard
archetypes.description: Fill up your graveyard, then use harmonize, renew, or other methods to get bonuses for cards leaving your graveyard.
archetypes.strategy: Grindy control. Get value when you delve or reanimate cards. Hardest archetype to draft but highest ceiling.

general_tips: Dragonstorm is a high-synergy set. A deck of 'good cards' will often lose to a well-tuned Flurry or Mobilize deck.
Don't ignore the mana fixing. In a set with 3-color clans, you need at least 2-3 dual lands or Tarkir Wilds to be consistent.
Harmonize is the best way to catch up from behind. Doubling a removal spell can completely flip the board state.
Watch out for the 'Leaves the Graveyard' deck; it can exile your key Renew targets before you can use them.
Tokens are everywhere. Cards that deal damage to all small creatures are premium sideboard options.
